_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies DE as 'non-photo ID requested' — voters provide name and address; poll workers may ask for a document showing name and address but it is not strictly required. Delaware does not have early in-person voting but offers no-excuse absentee voting. 2026 primary date (Sept 12) follows standard DE schedule; TODO verify exact 2026 date. Registration deadline is 24 days before the election per DE law.

earlyVoting.notes: Delaware does not offer traditional early in-person voting. No-excuse absentee (mail) voting is available for all registered voters.
Delaware does not offer traditional early in-person voting. No-excuse absentee (mail) voting is available for all registered voters.
Delaware does not offer traditional early in-person voting. No-excuse absentee voting (mail or in-person at county election offices) is available for all registered voters.

acceptedIds: Any document showing name and address (utility bill, bank statement, paycheck, etc.)
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Delaware does not have a statewide ban on phones in polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot or interfere with other voters. No electioneering within 50 feet of a polling place.
additionalRules: Non-photo ID requested but not required. Poll workers may ask for a document showing name and address.
Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. Unregistered or independent voters may not participate.
No-excuse absentee voting available; voters may also return ballots in person at county election offices.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 4 days before the election (received by county department of elections)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county department of elections by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county election office by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
